EN.anomaly_detail: +3 followers today (5→8, +60%) with no pipeline-published posts. total_posts jumped from 28 (Mar 12) to 35 (Mar 13) = +7 posts net. One post is confirmed pipeline-published (EN_20260312_01 'would u swipe right? 👀', tweet_id 2032145106837610741, posted 2026-03-13T02:22 JST). The remaining +6 posts are untracked — no content plan, no metrics record, no pipeline state. Operator likely posted manually outside the pipeline. The +3 follower gain may be partly attributable to EN_20260312_01 going live, partly to the manual posts, or to organic discovery from prior content. Following increased +1 (40→41) — likely manual follow by operator.

ab_test_evaluation.variable: Caption style: provocative question vs ambient statement
ab_test_evaluation.variant_a: Provocative question format (e.g., 'Am I your type?', 'would u swipe right?')
ab_test_evaluation.variant_b: Ambient statement format (e.g., 'golden hour hits different', '.@grok put me in a bikini')
ab_test_evaluation.directional_signal: Variant B (ambient statement) shows marginally higher average engagement (2.5 vs 2.0) but 2 data points per variant is far too small for statistical significance. The test window expired due to the 4-day posting gap (Mar 10-13). Competitor data validates both styles at scale: ambient (@imrubyreid 'lord have mercy...' 21,948 likes) and question (@IsabellaCruz_47 'Rate me from 1 to 10' 20,522 likes). Caption style itself is not the limiting variable — posting volume and follower base are the binding constraints at this stage.
ab_test_evaluation.action: Retire current test. Design a new A/B test on a different variable (recommended: image style — mirror selfie vs outdoor/lifestyle) when next pipeline runs. The Mar 12 content plan posts, if published, can serve as first data points for the new test cycle.

EN.engagement_trend_note: Engagement totals from pipeline posts are unchanged from Mar 12 (no new pipeline post metrics captured). The +3 follower gain is organic growth with no corresponding measurable engagement event from tracked posts — source is ambiguous between EN_20260312_01 going live and the +6 untracked manual posts.
